hero.tagline: Transforming ideas into exceptional digital experiences

about.title: About
about.description: Passionate Full-Stack developer with expertise in creating modern web and mobile applications. I transform complex concepts into elegant and high-performance digital solutions.

medAiHiring.title: Full Stack Developer
medAiHiring.description: Built a medical AI system and the associated website / medical interface, with a focus on fast iteration, reliability, and a clear user experience.

estateUp.title: Founder & Full Stack Developer
estateUp.description: Development of a web application for AI-powered real estate photo enhancement: virtual home staging, blue sky, retouches. Modern stack (React, TypeScript, Django, Stripe, Supabase) with ultra-realistic rendering and a simple user experience in just a few clicks.

arcCycle.title: Founder & Developer
arcCycle.description: Creation of a mobile application with habit tracking, random daily challenges, a motivating gamification system, and advanced statistics to track your progress.

mngrsAi.title: Full Stack Developer
mngrsAi.description: Contribution to the development of an innovative web platform using artificial intelligence to support music artists in their creative process and complete career management.

consultant.title: Consultant / Developer
consultant.description: Versatile consultant working on various projects: software development, web applications, technical solutions and DevOps infrastructure setup for different clients.

freelance.title: Full Stack Freelance Developer
freelance.description: Occasionally alongside my studies, I worked as a freelance developer for various clients, mainly showcase websites and personal e-commerce stores.

arcCycle.description: Arc Cycle is a mobile app built to develop daily discipline. Users start an "arc" — a multi-day cycle focused on a theme (fitness, wellness, productivity…) with concrete goals. Every day the app generates a random challenge — meditation, workout, reading — to break routine. A habit tracker with streaks, progression charts and a personal balance radar accompanies the user. Every action earns XP to level up and unlock achievements. The app also includes motivational quotes, an iOS widget, and deep customization (icons, wallpapers, themes). Available in French and English.
arcCycle.short: Arc Cycle is a React Native mobile app designed to help you develop personal discipline through a system of progressive cycles (arcs), daily challenges, and habit tracking.
arcCycle.screens_items: Welcome Screen — Animated app presentation and multi-step onboarding to set up your profile|Habits Tab — Create custom habits by category, track daily streaks and visualize your progression|Motivation Tab — Browse inspirational quotes, save favorites and customize the wallpaper|Profile Tab — Personal stats, level and XP, unlocked achievements and balance radar|Settings — Notification preferences, app icon selection, Premium subscription management and language settings|iOS Widget — Configurable home screen widget displaying a favorite motivational quote
arcCycle.features_items: Arc System — Progressive themed cycles with specific goals to achieve over multiple days|Daily Challenges — Random generation of varied challenges (fitness, meditation, wellness, creativity) with XP rewards|Habit Tracking — Unlimited habit creation with categories, consecutive streaks and notification reminders|Gamification — Experience points, leveling system, unlockable achievements and detailed statistics|Progression Charts — Visualize growth with curves, personal balance radar and full history|Smart Notifications — Configurable reminders for each habit and for daily challenges|Internationalization — Full interface in French and English with automatic language detection
arcCycle.premium_items: Per-habit custom notifications with configurable schedules|Custom wallpaper for quotes and widget background color|Extra challenge categories and exclusive challenges|Additional app icons and exclusive themes
arcCycle.learned_items: React Native & Expo — Cross-platform mobile development, lifecycle management, smooth animations with Reanimated|App Store Publishing — Full submission process, Apple review, metadata management via App Store Connect|Supabase — Backend as a Service: authentication, real-time database, Edge functions and SQL migrations|RevenueCat — In-app subscription integration, receipt handling and entitlement management|Sentry & Mixpanel — Production error monitoring, user analytics and key event tracking|Native iOS Widget — Swift widget development with bridging header for React Native ↔ Swift communication|Notifications — Full local and push notification management on iOS and Android, permissions and user preferences

estateUp.description: EstateUp is a platform that lets real estate professionals and individuals transform their property photos using artificial intelligence: virtual home staging, blue sky, brightening, decluttering and more. The goal is to showcase properties with brighter, more engaging photos — without reshooting or moving furniture. AI applies transformations on demand with consistent, professional results. Images are organized by projects (property, assignment) and teamwork is supported through organizations with shared credits.
estateUp.short: Enhance your real estate photos in 3 clicks: virtual home staging, blue sky, retouches... Ultra simple to use and ultra realistic rendering. Showcase your listings in seconds thanks to AI.
estateUp.howItWorks_items: Sign Up / Log In — Create an account with welcome credits, or log in to access the dashboard directly|Upload a Photo — From the dashboard, upload one or more property photos (interior, exterior, empty or cluttered room)|Choose Transformation — Select the desired effect (furnish, improve sky, brighten, declutter, change decor) with room type and style. Each transformation costs credits|Result — The transformed image is generated and available in your space, downloadable and assignable to a project|Organization — Sort images by project, review transformations, manage credits and collaborate via team organizations
estateUp.dashboard_items: Home — Overview with available credits, credits used, photos transformed and active projects. Quick access to recent albums and quick actions|My Projects — List of folders grouping images by property or assignment. Create, open, filter by date and shareable URL per project|Upload — 3-step process: upload + project selection, transformation configuration (type, room, decor style), confirmation and result access|Transformations — Project grid with preview and photo count. Dedicated section for unclassified images to sort later|Organization — Team management: create organizations, manage members and roles (owner, admin, member), shared credits for agencies and teams|Settings — Edit profile (name, email, password), theme preferences (light / dark)|Credits — Current balance, usage history and pack or plan purchase to continue transformations
estateUp.transforms_items: Virtual Home Staging — Furnish an empty space with realistic AI-generated furniture|Sky Enhancement — Replace a grey sky with a bright blue sky|Brightening — Improve the lighting of a dark room|Decluttering — Remove unnecessary objects to clean up the image|Combinations — Declutter + furnish, declutter + renovate, or all three at once|Decor Change — Switch the decoration style: modern, Scandinavian, industrial, classic, minimalist, bohemian. Applicable to living room, kitchen, bedroom, bathroom, office or dining room
estateUp.learned_items: Full-Stack Architecture — Designing a complete app with a React/TypeScript frontend and Django/Python backend communicating via REST API|AI Integration — Driving artificial intelligence models for real-time image generation and transformation|Stripe — Building a credit system with payment, pack management and consumption tracking|Supabase & PostgreSQL — Relational database, authentication, file storage and real-time features|Organization Management — Multi-user system with roles, permissions and shared credits across team members|Sentry — Production error monitoring on both frontend and backend|Deployment — CI/CD with Vercel for the frontend and backend infrastructure, environment variable management and migrations

jsonHelper.description: JSON Helper lets you visualize, explore and edit JSON data instantly. Raw JSON becomes an interactive visual graph so you can focus on structure and values without getting lost in brackets. Whether you are debugging an API response, reviewing a configuration file or exploring a dataset, you get a real-time visual representation of your data.
jsonHelper.short: Visualize, explore and edit JSON in real time: interactive tree graph, synced editor, split / code / graph modes, search, undo/redo and locally persisted session.
jsonHelper.features_items: Interactive tree visualization — JSON rendered as a navigable node graph with clear parent-child relationships|Live editing — Change values and keys from the graph or the built-in code editor; everything stays in sync instantly|Split, editor & visualizer modes — Side-by-side, code only or graph only|Search & filter — Quickly find any key or value across the structure|Undo / Redo — Every change is tracked and fully reversible|Zoom & pan — Move through large documents with smooth zoom and drag|Horizontal & vertical layouts — Switch tree orientation to fit your data|Persistent session — Work is saved locally so you do not lose progress
jsonHelper.audience_items: Developers — Debugging API responses or config files|QA engineers — Inspecting test payloads|Data analysts — Exploring structured datasets|Anyone — Who works with JSON and wants a clearer view than raw text
jsonHelper.gettingStarted_items: Paste or type your JSON into the editor — the visualization updates in real time. That is it.
jsonHelper.learned_items: Data visualization — Representing JSON as an interactive graph with zoom, pan and navigation|State synchronization — Keeping a text editor and graph view consistent in real time|Developer-tool UX — Multiple layout modes, search, undo/redo stack and local persistence for a smooth workflow|Web stack — Vite, React and TypeScript app deployed on Vercel

bleroy's life.description: Bleroy's Life is an interactive simulation of Conway's Game of Life as a web app. Draw by hand, place classic shapes (gliders, pulsars, methuselahs…), run the simulation at any speed, zoom and navigate a 1000×1000 cell grid, then save your creations locally in the browser. Perfect for discovering the Game of Life, testing configurations or simply watching patterns evolve.
bleroy's life.short: Conway's Game of Life, web version. Create, watch and save your patterns on an infinite grid, with a smooth interface and a polished intro.
bleroy's life.features_items: 1000×1000 Grid — Centered view on load with automatic zoom on the visible area|Navigation — Pan with scroll wheel or right-click, zoom with wheel or +/− buttons|Editing — Draw and erase on click, rectangular selection, copy-paste patterns|Simulation — Play / pause with real-time speed adjustment|Saving — Save patterns and folders, full persistence in the browser (localStorage)|Interface — Polished animated intro, dark theme, smooth animations with Motion and Radix UI components
bleroy's life.presets_items: Oscillators — Pulsar, Pentadecathlon, Blinker and other patterns that oscillate between states|Spaceships — Glider, Lightweight Spaceship (LWSS) and other shapes that move across the grid|Guns — Gosper's Glider Gun, capable of generating an infinite stream of gliders|Methuselahs — R-pentomino, Acorn and other patterns that evolve for a very long time before stabilizing|Still Lifes — Block, Beehive, Loaf and other stable shapes that never change|20+ Presets — All placeable with a single click, combinable to create complex configurations
bleroy's life.learned_items: Cellular Algorithms — Implementing Conway's Game of Life, efficiently managing a large grid (1000×1000) and optimizing rendering|Canvas & Performance — Performant rendering of thousands of cells with zoom, pan and real-time update management|Complex State Management — Synchronizing grid state, simulation, editing and saving without conflicts|Local Persistence — Save system with named folders and patterns in the browser's localStorage|Animations & UX — Animated intro with Motion, intuitive interface with Radix UI components and fluid interactions (draw, select, copy-paste)

linattendu.description: Modern and elegant showcase website for L'Inattendu, a romantic suite and love room. Visitors can discover the suite and amenities through a photo gallery, check rates by period (weekday/weekend) and time (day/night), then book directly via a contact form with automatic email sending. The site is fully responsive, SEO-optimized and deployed on Vercel.
linattendu.short: Website for a Loveroom with booking management and automated email system. Designed to simplify space booking, it offers a smooth user experience with real-time notifications and automatic email confirmations.
linattendu.pages_items: Hero — Main presentation showcasing the suite, tagline and call-to-action toward booking|Gallery — Responsive photo grid of the suite with hover effects, overlay and scroll animations|Pricing — Interactive selectors for period (weekday/weekend) and time (day/night), animated slider between options, dynamic price display and supplements list|Contact — Contact form with field validation, anti-spam protection (30s cooldown), email sending via EmailJS, date validation and toast notifications|Footer — Social media links (Facebook, Instagram, Snapchat, TikTok), quick section links and contact information
linattendu.features_items: Automated Email Sending — Contact form connected to EmailJS to send booking requests directly by email, with custom template (name, email, phone, date, message)|Smooth Navigation — Responsive menu with smooth scroll to sections and animated design with Motion|Dynamic Pricing — Interactive selector system that adapts prices in real time based on selected period and time slot|Responsive Design — Interface optimized for mobile, tablet and desktop with adapted breakpoints|Full SEO — Optimized meta tags, sitemap.xml, robots.txt, Open Graph tags for social sharing and manifest.json for PWA support|Security — Multiple submission protection (cooldown), field validation and environment variables for API keys
linattendu.learned_items: EmailJS — Client-side email service integration without a backend, template configuration and environment variable management|Scroll Animations — Using Motion to trigger smooth animations when sections appear in the viewport|Technical SEO — Complete SEO setup: sitemap, robots.txt, Open Graph meta tags and PWA manifest|Interactive Components — Building animated pricing selectors with dynamic states and smooth transitions|Form UX — Client-side validation, anti-spam protection, user feedback with toast notifications and date handling

aquapro.description: Professional showcase website for AquaPro, a trusted plumber in Montpellier and its metropolitan area. The site presents all services (24/7 emergency repairs, leak detection, water heaters, sanitary, unclogging, renovation), highlights the company's strengths (15 years of experience, 500+ satisfied clients, 30-min response time) and makes it easy to get in touch. Modern, responsive interface optimized for local SEO.
aquapro.short: Professional website for a plumber in Montpellier, built to showcase services and make getting in touch fast and easy.
aquapro.services_items: Emergency Repairs — Fast 24/7 response for emergencies: leaks, clogged pipes, water heater breakdowns|Leak Repair — Detection and repair using cutting-edge technology to locate hidden leaks|Water Heaters — Installation, repair and maintenance of electric, gas or thermodynamic water heaters|Sanitary — Full installation and renovation of bathrooms, toilets, sinks and sanitary equipment|Unclogging — Pipe, sink and toilet unclogging with hydro-jetting and camera inspection|Renovation — Complete plumbing renovation, code compliance and system modernization
aquapro.sections_items: Hero — Company presentation with tagline, emergency number and call-to-action to the contact form|Services — Detailed grid of 6 services with icons, descriptions and scroll animations|Advantages — Highlighted strengths: fast response (30 min), certified experts (15 years), 10-year warranty, 500+ clients (4.9/5), local team since 2008, modern equipment|Service Area — Coverage map across Montpellier Centre, Castelnau, Lattes, Pérols, Juvignac and the entire metropolitan area with free travel|Contact — Contact section with 24/7 emergency number and appointment booking form
aquapro.learned_items: Local SEO — Optimizing search rankings for a local tradesman: geo-targeted keywords, content structured for Google My Business and local searches|Effective Showcase Site — Designing a conversion-oriented site: clear visual hierarchy, visible call-to-actions and streamlined user journey toward contact|Responsive & Accessibility — Full mobile/tablet/desktop adaptation for a diverse audience, with mobile-friendly text sizes and buttons|Professional Animations — Using Motion for subtle scroll animations that reinforce credibility without weighing down the experience

streetshop.description: StreetShop is a premium CBD e-commerce store. The platform offers a full catalog (oils, flowers, resins, cosmetics, e-liquids), a cart system with secure Stripe payments, user authentication, order tracking and full legal compliance. The Node.js/Express backend with PostgreSQL handles products, orders and users, all containerized with Docker.
streetshop.short: CBD showcase and ordering website for flowers, oils, resins, cosmetics, infusions and e-liquids. A modern e-commerce platform with a smooth and secure user experience.
streetshop.products_items: CBD Oils — Various dosages and spectrums (full, broad, isolate) for every need|CBD Flowers — Hand-harvested premium flower selection with detailed product sheets|CBD Resins & Hash — Top-quality products with analysis certificates|CBD Cosmetics — Creams, balms and skincare products made with cannabidiol|Supplements — CBD capsules and pills with precise dosages|CBD E-liquids — Full range for vaping with different concentrations
streetshop.features_items: Catalog & Filters — Browse by category, advanced search and filters to quickly find the right product|Smart Cart — Add, edit, remove products with real-time total calculation and quantity management|Secure Stripe Payments — Full Stripe integration for secure credit card transactions|Authentication — Account creation, login, profile management and order history|Detailed Product Pages — Photos, full descriptions, dosages, usage tips and analysis certificates|REST API Backend — Node.js/Express server with PostgreSQL for product, order and user management|Docker — Containerized environment for development and deployment|Legal Compliance — Age verification (+18), THC < 0.2% labeling, terms of service and privacy policy
streetshop.learned_items: Full E-commerce — Designing an end-to-end purchase flow: catalog, cart, checkout, payment and order confirmation|Stripe — Payment system integration: session creation, webhook handling, transaction security and error management|Node.js/Express Backend — Building a complete REST API with CRUD routes, auth middleware, data validation and error handling|PostgreSQL — Relational database modeling: products, categories, users, orders and order lines|Authentication — Complete system with registration, login, JWT tokens, route protection and session management|Docker — Containerizing the application (frontend + backend + DB) for a reproducible development environment

bouncingBalls.description: You know those bouncing balls taking over TikTok that make people addicted? I decided to recreate this little phenomenon over a weekend with Python and Pygame. The program simulates bouncing balls on screen with realistic physics: gravity, edge bouncing, collisions. Each ball can display custom text, an image and play a sound on impact. Everything is configurable via command-line arguments.
bouncingBalls.short: You know those bouncing balls taking over TikTok that make people addicted? Well, I decided to recreate this little phenomenon over a weekend.
bouncingBalls.features_items: Physics Simulation — Gravity, velocity, realistic edge bouncing and ball-to-ball collision detection|Multiple Balls — Up to 5 simultaneous balls, each with its own behavior and trajectory|Custom Text — Each ball can display a different message right on it|Ball Images — Optional image support for a more visual rendering|Impact Sounds — Sound effects on each bounce, toggleable on/off|CLI Configuration — Everything is set via command line: number of balls, texts, window title
bouncingBalls.customization_items: --balls — Number of balls to display (1 to 5)|--text1 to --text5 — Custom text for each ball|--title — Game window title|Images — Add an image per ball for a custom visual|Sounds — Enable sound effects on each bounce via code
bouncingBalls.learned_items: Pygame — Building a graphical application with render loop, event handling and sprite display|Simulation Physics — Implementing gravity, velocity, elastic bouncing and collision detection|CLI Arguments — Command-line argument parsing with argparse to make the program configurable|Sound Management — Integrating pygame.mixer to play sounds on each collision and impact

learning.title: Learning
learning.description: I love learning: I've tried data analysis, 2D and 3D games, binary trees, sorting algorithms and everything I can get my hands on. Browse my GitHub, even though 90% of my projects are private or licensed, there are still some gems from my beginnings to explore.
learning.short: I love learning: I've tried data analysis, 2D and 3D games, binary trees, sorting algorithms and everything I can get my hands on.

ftTranscendance.description: 42 final team project: a complete multiplayer website built from scratch to play Pong online. The project goes well beyond the game — it includes a full authentication system (OAuth2 + 2FA), real-time chat with channels and direct messages, a ranking system, friends and invitation notifications, all in a responsive single page application with dynamic content updates.
ftTranscendance.short: 42 final project: a complete multiplayer site with online Pong, OAuth2 + 2FA auth, real-time chat and ranking system.
ftTranscendance.features_items: Real-time Pong — Multiplayer Pong game in an HTML5 canvas, responsive, with matchmaking and network play via Socket.io|Full Authentication — OAuth2 login, two-factor authentication (2FA), JWT tokens and password encryption|Real-time Chat — Direct messages between users and instant discussion channels with Socket.io|User Profiles — Account creation, avatar, game statistics, match history and privacy settings|Ranking & Friends — Victory-based ranking system, friends list, user blocking and invitation notifications|Privacy Management — Privacy settings, user blocking and personal data control|Responsive SPA — Single page application with dynamic content updates, no page reloads
ftTranscendance.stack_items: NestJS Backend — TypeScript REST API with NestJS, route handling, auth middleware and business logic|Svelte Frontend — Svelte/TypeScript user interface, SPA with dynamic navigation and CSS animations|PostgreSQL Database — User, game, message and ranking storage via Prisma ORM|Socket.io — Bidirectional real-time communication for the Pong game and instant chat|Docker — Fully containerized project (frontend + backend + DB) with Docker Compose
ftTranscendance.learned_items: Teamwork — Group collaboration on a complex full-stack project with task distribution, code review and Git conflict management|REST API — Designing and implementing a complete RESTful API with authentication, validation and error handling|Web Security — JWT, password encryption, form validation, OAuth2 and two-factor authentication (2FA)|Socket.io — WebSocket integration for real-time: synchronized multiplayer game and instant chat|Prisma ORM — Relational database modeling, migrations and typed queries with Prisma|Docker Compose — Orchestrating multiple containerized services (frontend, backend, database) in a unified environment|UX/UI — Designing intuitive user interfaces with CSS animations and smooth SPA experience

ftContainer.description: 42 project consisting in reimplementing from scratch the C++ STL containers: vector, map, stack and set. Each container faithfully reproduces the behavior of its standard equivalent — same methods, same iterators, same complexity guarantees. The project also includes the required utilities (pair, enable_if, is_integral, iterator_traits…) and a Red-Black tree structure for map and set. Everything is tested comparatively against the original STL containers.
ftContainer.short: From-scratch reimplementation of C++ STL containers (vector, map, stack, set) with Red-Black tree and comparative tests.
ftContainer.containers_items: vector — Dynamic array with contiguous allocation, automatic resizing (capacity/size), random access iterators, and all standard methods (push_back, insert, erase, resize, reserve…)|map — Ordered key-value associative container, based on a self-balancing Red-Black tree. Bidirectional iterators, O(log n) lookup, insert, erase, find, lower/upper_bound|set — Ordered unique elements container, also based on a Red-Black tree. Same interface as map but without associated values|stack — LIFO (Last In First Out) container adapter, using vector as the default underlying container. Push, pop, top, empty, size
ftContainer.architecture_items: Iterators/ — Custom iterators (random_access_iterator, bidirectional_iterator, reverse_iterator) with all traits and operators required by the STL|Trees/ — Red-Black tree implementation (self-balancing binary search tree) used by map and set to guarantee O(log n) operations|Utilities/ — Template tools: pair, make_pair, enable_if, is_integral, equal, lexicographical_compare and iterator_traits|tests/ — Test suite comparing the behavior of each ft:: container with its std:: equivalent to validate conformity
ftContainer.learned_items: C++ Templates — Advanced generic programming: class templates, partial specialization, SFINAE with enable_if and type traits|Data Structures — Deep understanding of vector internals (allocation, reallocation, iterator invalidation), Red-Black trees (rotations, rebalancing, insertion/deletion) and stack implementation|Iterators — Designing STL-compliant iterators across all categories (random access, bidirectional), with traits and standard algorithm compatibility|Algorithmic Complexity — Respecting STL complexity guarantees: amortized O(1) for vector::push_back, O(log n) for map::find, etc.|Memory Management — Allocators, in-place object construction/destruction, capacity vs size management and memory leak prevention

cub3d.description: My first real game. 42 pair project directly inspired by Wolfenstein 3D: recreating a 3D raycasting engine from scratch in C with MiniLibX. The player moves in first-person view through a maze defined by a .cub file, with textured wall rendering, floor and ceiling management, and a minimap. The project also includes a bonus version with extra features.
cub3d.short: My first game: a 3D raycasting engine in C inspired by Wolfenstein 3D, with textures, minimap and collisions.
cub3d.features_items: 3D First-Person View — Real-time rendering of a 3D environment from a 2D map using raycasting, just like Wolfenstein 3D|Player Movement — Forward/backward movement, left/right strafing and camera rotation via keyboard, with wall collision handling|Wall Textures — Each wall face (North, South, East, West) can have a different texture, applied with correct distance-based projection|Floor & Ceiling — Configurable colors for floor and ceiling via the .cub configuration file|Minimap — Overlay mini-map showing the player's position and maze structure|Map Parsing — Reading and validating a .cub file defining the map (walls, spaces, starting position), textures and colors|Bonus Version — Bonus folder with additional features beyond the base subject
cub3d.technical_items: Raycasting — For each pixel column on screen, a ray is cast from the player's position. The DDA (Digital Differential Analyzer) algorithm detects wall intersections and calculates distance to determine the height of the wall strip to draw|MiniLibX — 42's minimalist graphics library for opening a window, drawing pixel by pixel and handling keyboard events. All rendering is done manually, with no graphics engine|.cub File — Configuration format defining texture paths (NO, SO, WE, EA), floor (F) and ceiling (C) RGB colors, and the maze map as characters (1 = wall, 0 = empty, N/S/E/W = player position and orientation)|Mathematics — Trigonometry for ray direction, corrected distances (fish-eye fix), perspective projection and texture mapping onto surfaces
cub3d.learned_items: Raycasting — Understanding and implementing the DDA algorithm for ray casting, wall/ray intersection calculation and fish-eye distortion correction|Low-Level Graphics Programming — Pixel-by-pixel rendering without a graphics engine, framebuffer management, 60 FPS display and in-memory image manipulation|Applied Mathematics — Trigonometry (sin, cos, tan), direction vectors, camera plane, perspective projection and 2D coordinate → texture mapping|C Memory Management — Proper allocation and deallocation of all resources (textures, map, structures), with no memory leaks|Pair Programming — Task splitting (parsing + rendering), Git coordination and collaborative problem solving

pushswap.description: 42 project that requires sorting data on a stack with a limited instruction set, in as few moves as possible. You have two stacks (A and B) and only 11 available operations. The challenge: find and implement the most efficient sorting algorithm to sort any list of numbers while meeting performance constraints (under 700 operations for 100 numbers, under 5500 for 500).
pushswap.short: Sorting algorithm on two stacks with only 11 possible operations, in as few moves as possible.
pushswap.operations_items: sa / sb / ss — Swap: swap the first two elements at the top of stack A, B, or both at once|pa / pb — Push: take the top element from one stack and place it on top of the other|ra / rb / rr — Rotate: shift all elements up (first element becomes last)|rra / rrb / rrr — Reverse Rotate: shift all elements down (last element becomes first)
pushswap.algorithm_items: Small cases (3-5 elements) — Hard-coded sorting with optimal operation sequences for 2, 3, 4 and 5 elements|Medium and large cases — Chunk sort algorithm: stack A is split into groups (chunks) sorted by value range, pushed to B, then reinserted into A in the correct order|Indexing — Each number is replaced by its index in the sorted list, simplifying comparisons and chunk splitting|Move cost optimization — Calculating the cost of each move (combination of A and B rotations) to pick the cheapest displacement at each step|Performance targets — Under 700 operations for 100 numbers, under 5500 for 500 numbers
pushswap.learned_items: Sorting Algorithms — Studying and comparing different algorithms (insertion, radix, chunk sort) to find the best fit for the project's constraints|Algorithmic Complexity — Analyzing operation count relative to input size, optimizing to stay under imposed thresholds|Data Structures — Stack manipulation (linked lists) with limited operations, index management and value range handling|Optimization — Reducing move count by calculating the real cost of each movement and combining simultaneous operations (ss, rr, rrr)|C Parsing & Error Handling — Argument validation (duplicates, overflow, invalid characters), clean error handling and memory management

rainfall.description: Rainfall is a 42 computer security challenge, more complex than Snow Crash, that dives deep into reverse engineering. The concept: a VM ISO containing compiled binaries, one per level (level0 to level9 + bonus). For each level, you must analyze the binary with GDB, reconstruct the C/C++ source code, understand its logic, identify the vulnerability and exploit it to obtain the next level's password. Each successful exploit grants access to the next system user.
rainfall.short: 42 security challenge: binary reverse engineering and vulnerability exploitation (buffer overflow, format string, heap…) level by level.
rainfall.concept_items: Isolated VM — The challenge takes place in a dedicated ISO virtual machine. Each level is a system user with its own SUID binary|Reverse Engineering — For each level, the binary must be disassembled and analyzed with GDB to reconstruct the original source code and understand the program logic|Exploitation — Once the vulnerability is identified, a payload (malicious input) must be crafted to exploit it and execute arbitrary code or read the password|Progression — Each exploit yields the next level's flag (password), allowing login as the next-level user
rainfall.levels_items: Buffer Overflow — Stack buffer overflow to overwrite the return address and redirect execution to a shellcode or target function|Format String — Exploiting printf without a format string to read and write memory at arbitrary addresses via %x, %n and %s|Heap Overflow — Heap buffer overflow to corrupt allocation metadata and hijack execution flow|Use After Free — Exploiting pointers to freed memory to control data and redirect execution|Integer Overflow — Integer value overflow to bypass size checks and trigger unexpected behavior|Shellcode Injection — Injecting machine code into program memory and redirecting execution via NOP sled techniques
rainfall.learned_items: GDB & Disassembly — Advanced GDB usage: breakpoints, stack and register inspection, function disassembly, step-by-step instruction execution|x86 Architecture — Understanding stack mechanics (stack frames, return addresses, EBP/ESP), calling conventions and virtual memory|Binary Exploitation — Exploitation techniques: buffer overflow, format string attacks, heap corruption, ret2libc, shellcode injection and protection bypassing|Reverse Engineering — Reconstructing C/C++ code from assembly, identifying data structures, loops and conditions in disassembly|Systems Security — Understanding protections (ASLR, NX bit, stack canaries) and their limitations, awareness of common C/C++ vulnerabilities

inception.description: 42 system administration project that consists in building a complete infrastructure with Docker. Each service runs in its own dedicated container, built from a custom Dockerfile (no pre-built images). Everything is orchestrated with Docker Compose in a virtual machine. The result: a working WordPress site served by NGINX over HTTPS, with a MariaDB database, all connected via an internal Docker network and persistent volumes.
inception.short: Complete Docker infrastructure: NGINX + WordPress + MariaDB, all custom Dockerfiles orchestrated with Docker Compose.
inception.services_items: NGINX — Web server and reverse proxy, the only entry point to the infrastructure. Configured with TLS v1.2/v1.3 to serve the site over HTTPS only on port 443|WordPress + php-fpm — WordPress CMS installed and configured automatically via shell scripts, with php-fpm for PHP request processing. Two WordPress users are created at startup (including one administrator)|MariaDB — Relational database for WordPress, automatically initialized with database creation, user setup and permissions. Data persisted via a Docker volume
inception.architecture_items: Custom Dockerfiles — Each service has its own Dockerfile written from scratch, based on Debian/Alpine. No pre-built Docker Hub images are used|Docker Compose — docker-compose.yml file orchestrating all 3 services, the internal network and volumes. A single docker-compose up launches the entire infrastructure|Docker Network — Isolated bridge network connecting containers. Only NGINX is exposed externally, WordPress and MariaDB are only accessible from the internal network|Persistent Volumes — Two volumes: one for WordPress files (themes, plugins, uploads) and one for MariaDB data. Data survives container restarts|TLS/HTTPS — Auto-generated self-signed SSL certificate to serve the site over HTTPS. NGINX configured with TLS v1.2 and v1.3 protocols only|Virtual Machine — The entire project must run inside a VM, simulating an isolated production environment|Makefile — Build and launch automation with make (build, up, down, clean, re)
inception.learned_items: Docker In-Depth — Writing multi-stage Dockerfiles, build cache management, best practices (one process per container, lightweight images, no root)|Docker Compose — Orchestrating interdependent services, startup dependency management, network and volume configuration|System Administration — Configuring NGINX as a reverse proxy, TLS/SSL management, MariaDB administration and automated WordPress installation|Shell Scripts — Full automation of service initialization: database creation, WordPress setup, SSL certificate generation|Networking & Security — Service isolation via Docker networks, minimal exposure (single port), secure inter-container communication|Data Persistence — Docker volume management for persistence, distinguishing between ephemeral and persistent data

pythonDataScience.description: 42 Python Data Science pool, organized over 5 days (Day00 to Day04). Each day covers a data science aspect in Python: data manipulation, CSV file reading, visualization with charts and image display, all in pure Python with the data ecosystem libraries (Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib). The project is 100% Python.
pythonDataScience.short: 42 Data Science pool: data manipulation, CSV, visualization with Matplotlib and analysis with Pandas/NumPy.
pythonDataScience.days_items: Day00 — Python Introduction: language basics, data types, loops, functions and string manipulation|Day01 — Data Manipulation: CSV file reading and parsing, data cleaning, filtering and transformation with native Python structures|Day02 — NumPy & Arrays: array operations, vectorized calculations, basic statistics (mean, median, standard deviation) and matrix manipulation|Day03 — Visualization: creating charts with Matplotlib (line plots, histograms, scatter plots, bar charts), axis and legend customization, image display|Day04 — Pandas & Analysis: DataFrames, CSV dataset loading, advanced filtering, aggregation, groupby and exploratory data analysis
pythonDataScience.learned_items: Python for Data — Using Python in a data science context, analysis and data processing scripts|CSV File Reading — CSV parsing with native Python and Pandas, raw data cleaning and structuring|Data Visualization — Creating various charts with Matplotlib: line plots, histograms, scatter plots, image display and visual customization|NumPy — Array manipulation, performant vectorized calculations and basic statistical operations|Pandas — DataFrames, dataset loading, filtering, aggregation and tabular data exploration

querytest.description: Internal data visualization tool: ask a question in natural language, an LLM (OpenAI or Ollama) generates the corresponding SQL query, it's executed on PostgreSQL and results are displayed as a table, line chart, bar chart or pie chart. The project is a pnpm monorepo with a Next.js frontend and NestJS backend, connected to PostgreSQL via Drizzle ORM. Reports can be saved for reuse.
querytest.short: Ask a question in natural language, an LLM generates the SQL and results are displayed as tables and charts.
querytest.howItWorks_items: Natural Language Question — The user types a question (e.g. "List of doctors", "Number of visits per patient") in the Next.js interface|SQL Generation by LLM — The question is sent to the NestJS backend which forwards it to an LLM (OpenAI or local Ollama) with the PostgreSQL schema as context. The LLM generates the corresponding SQL query|Security — Only SELECT queries are allowed. Any query containing DROP, DELETE, INSERT, UPDATE or TRUNCATE is rejected|PostgreSQL Execution — The SQL is executed via Drizzle ORM on the PostgreSQL database. On error, the system retries up to 3 times, sending the error message to the LLM for correction|Result Display — Data is shown as table, line chart, bar chart or pie chart via Recharts, with the option to save the report
querytest.features_items: Multi-format Visualization — Table, line chart, bar chart and pie chart to display results based on data type|Report Saving — Save queries with name, generated SQL and view type for reuse via a sidebar|DB Stats — Toggle to display database counts (doctors, patients, visits, etc.) and verify result consistency|Auto-corrections — Post-processing of generated SQL: backtick removal, comma fixes, aggregate relocation, complex pattern rewriting (CTE + ROW_NUMBER)|Smart Retry — On PostgreSQL error, automatic retry with error message sent to the LLM to guide correction (up to 3 attempts)|pnpm Monorepo — Monorepo architecture with pnpm workspace separating the NestJS API and Next.js frontend|Demo Data — Complete seed with organizations, facilities, doctors, patients, insurances and 250+ visits
querytest.learned_items: LLM Integration — Connecting to an LLM (OpenAI / Ollama) for natural language to SQL generation, prompt engineering with database schema as context|NestJS — Building a structured backend API with modules (query, reports, stats), services, controllers and exception filters|Next.js — React frontend with routing, API proxy (2-min timeout for long LLM calls), and data visualization interface|Drizzle ORM — PostgreSQL schema modeling, migrations, data seeding and typed TypeScript queries|SQL Post-processing — Writing automatic fixes to make LLM-generated SQL more reliable (regex patterns, complex query rewriting)|Recharts — Dynamic data visualization with different chart types adapted to returned results

abregedin.description: LinkedIn-related project: automation tool for summarizing professional profiles. Simplifies the extraction and synthesis of key information from the platform.

reviews.title: Reviews
reviews.subtitle: What my clients say about my work

items.name: Anthony
items.comment: Benoit showed remarkable responsiveness and adaptability. The deadline was short almost unrealistic: he made himself available for an interview in under 48 hours, with the completed technical assignment. He delivered very efficiently and integrated well despite the time difference.
Benoit integrated well with the team and showed strong initiative by proposing interesting and innovative ideas.

items.name: Adrien
items.comment: Benoit was extremely responsive and attentive to our needs for our family garage. He immediately understood what we wanted and delivered effective solutions. His professionalism and kindness truly made the difference.

Benoit delivered a design very quickly and guided us throughout the project. Once the website was revamped and SEO was set up, it generated dozens of visits and bookings starting on the second day after launch.

items.name: Arc Cycle user
items.comment: Clean, well-crafted app — the best I’ve tried for habits and motivation. Can’t wait to see what’s next!

items.name: Julien
items.comment: Benoit helped us migrate our website to a new language and a new platform. He was highly professional and adapted to our needs.
